items.title: Morningstar DBRS flags worsening private credit quality as downgrades increase
items.summary: USA-DEBT/:Morningstar DBRS flags worsening private credit quality as downgrades increase

items.title: Software, Payment Shares Sink After Citrini Post on AI Risks
items.summary: Delivery, payments, and software stocks slid sharply Monday after Citrini Research published a report laying out the potential risks that artificial intelligence could pose to various segments of the global economy.

items.title: European Stocks Fall as Tariff Confusion Returns; Novo Slumps
items.summary: European stocks fell as President Donald Trump’s latest tariff moves sowed fresh confusion over the outlook for global trade. Shares in Novo Nordisk A/S slumped after its next-generation obesity shot drug fell short of Eli Lilly & Co.’s rival blockbuster in a trial.

items.title: PNGS Reva IPO: Diamond jewellery firm raises  ₹171 crore from anchor investors ahead of public issue
items.summary: PNGS Reva collects nearly  ₹171 cr from anchor investors; IPO opens on Tuesday

items.title: PNGS Reva IPO: Citigroup, Societe Generale back jeweller in Rs 170 crore anchor round
items.summary: PNGS Reva Diamond Jewellery raised Rs 170.58 crore from anchor investors ahead of its IPO opening on February 24. Global institutions like Citigroup Global Markets Mauritius and Société Générale, along with domestic mutual funds, participated, providing early support for the Rs 380 crore IPO through the book-building route.

items.title: Zerodha's Nithin Kamath explains how depository participant fees affect stock market investors — What you need to know
items.summary: Zerodha CEO Nithin Kamath explains what depository participant (DP) charges are and how broking firms impose the fee on stock market investors when they sell their stock holdings. Here's what you need to know about the hidden charge.&nbsp;

items.title: US Markets | Domino's shares jump 5% as US sales beat estimates
items.summary: Domino's Pizza beat Wall Street estimates ​for fourth-quarter U.S. same-store sales on Monday, as value-driven promotions and new menu items fueled demand, sending the pizza chain's shares up about 5% in early trading.

items.title: Ahead of Market: 10 things that will decide stock market action on Tuesday
items.summary: Indian markets enter Tuesday’s session with momentum after gains in the Sensex and Nifty, but analysts say direction will hinge on key resistance levels, volatility trends in India VIX and cues from global developments following the US Supreme Court’s tariff ruling involving Donald Trump.

items.title: Precious Metal Prices | FM Sitharaman downplays gold surge as central bank buying spree continues
Gold, silver rates today: Comex gold jumps $140/oz; silver gains $5.5/oz on weak dollar, tariff uncertainty
items.summary: Gold and silver prices surged on 23 February following a US Supreme Court ruling against Trump's tariffs, making dollar-priced commodities more appealing. Gold futures reached $5,198, while silver prices rose significantly, indicating strong market interest despite tariff uncertainties.

items.title: European oil and gas stocks hit record high, surpassing 2007 level
items.summary: The ​European ​oil and gas stocks ​index hit a record high on Monday, surpassing ‌a ⁠previous record hit ⁠in 2007, ​helped in recent weeks by ​a rise in the price of oil.

items.title: Sebi chief Tuhin Kanta Pandey flags big PMS overhaul, to examine new RBI funding rules
items.summary: Sebi chief Tuhin Kanta Pandey said the regulator plans a comprehensive overhaul of PMS rules to improve suitability, governance, and conduct, while also examining industry representations on the RBI’s new capital-market funding framework. He added that Sebi is also working on oversight for to-be-listed stocks.

items.title: Dow Jones, S&P 500, Nasdaq drop after Donald Trump's new 15% US tariff update — Here's what investors need to know
items.summary: Dow Jones, S&amp;P 500, and Nasdaq dropped during the trading session on 23 February 2026, following US President Donald Trump's 15% US tariff announcement over the weekend. Here's what investors need to know.

items.title: Market Trading Guide: Tourism Finance and Cummins India are stocks to buy on Tuesday for up to 16% returns. Here’s why
items.summary: Tourism Finance Corporation and Cummins India are top buy picks for Tuesday with up to 16% potential returns backed by strong technical breakouts.

items.title: Nashik civic body announces launch of green bonds to finance Simhastha Kumbh Mela projects
items.summary: Nashik Municipal Corporation on Monday announced the launch of its maiden public issue of green bonds, which officials said would finance several projects associated with the 2027 Simhastha Kumbh Mela.

items.title: US stock markets today: US stocks decline with tech leading losses as tariff uncertainty weighs
items.summary: US stocks slipped on Monday as tariff uncertainty resurfaced after former President Donald Trump announced a new levy following the US Supreme Court ruling, pressuring the Dow Jones Industrial Average, S&P 500 and Nasdaq Composite while tech shares weakened ahead of key earnings.

items.title: Nexus Select Trust to pick 50% stake in Runwal Enterprises' upcoming mall in Mumbai for Rs 434 crore
items.summary: Realty firm Nexus Select Trust will acquire 50 per cent stake in an upcoming mall in Mumbai for Rs 434 crore as part of its expansion plan.

items.title: IPO-bound PNGS Reva Diamond Jewellery to invest Rs 287 cr to open 15 retail stores
items.summary: PNGS Reva Diamond Jewellery, which will hit the capital market on Tuesday with its maiden public issue, is planning to invest Rs 287 crore to set up 15 new retail stores to expand its business.

items.title: Will US tariff uncertainty boost near-term demand for gold and silver?
items.summary: Analysts suggest that gold prices may remain strong due to factors like geopolitical risks and US trade policy uncertainty, following the Supreme Court's ruling against President Trump's tariffs. Demand for precious metals is expected to rise until clarity on tariffs emerges.

items.title: ‘Pure tariff chaos.’ The world—and markets—react to Trump’s trade shift.
items.summary: President Donald Trump’s new 15% global tariff is causing uncertainty and confusion among investors and international lawmakers alike.

items.title: Carlsberg IPO soon? Beer maker plans to raise $700 million from Indian stock market, says report — All you need to know
items.summary: Carlsberg A/S is preparing for a possible $700 million IPO of its India unit, with advisers from Kotak Mahindra, JPMorgan, and Citigroup.&nbsp;

items.title: These 7 largecap stocks post over 50% EBITDA growth in December quarter
items.summary: IOC, Bajaj Holdings, Eternal, Muthoot Finance, Jio Financial, Titan and BPCL posted over 50% EBITDA growth in Q3 FY26, signalling strong operating momentum.

items.title: Bharti Airtel shares to be in focus after  ₹20,000 crore capital infusion in NBFC arm Airtel Money
items.summary: Bharti Airtel announced a  ₹20,000 crore capital infusion move into its non-banking financial company (NBFC) arm, Airtel Money. Here's what investors need to know about the company's stock and corporate update.

items.title: Market quote of the day by George Soros | “It’s not whether you’re right or wrong that’s important, but how much money you make when you’re right and how much you lose when you’re wrong.”
items.summary: George Soros’ philosophy emphasises that investing success isn’t about being right every time but about managing outcomes so gains outweigh losses. By focusing on asymmetric payoffs, disciplined risk management, and emotional resilience, investors can minimise downside, let winners compound, and build long-term positive expectancy despite market uncertainty.

items.title: Indian bonds tick higher as US-Iran jitters ease, but hefty debt sales loom
items.summary: Indian government bonds edged ​higher on Monday as geopolitical ​jitters ebbed ahead of a third round of ​U.S.-Iran nuclear talks, though heavy debt supply due this week capped gains.

items.title: Clean Max Enviro Energy Solutions IPO Day 1: Issue booked 34% amid strong QIB buying; check GMP and other details
items.summary: Clean Max Enviro Energy Solutions IPO was booked 34% on the first day of bidding. The  ₹3,100-crore IPO will remain open till February 25. The allotment will be finalised on February 26, and the listing will occur next week on March 2.

items.title: IDFC First Bank shares record worst crash since March 2020, end 16% lower
items.summary: IDFC First Bank ended 16% lower on Monday—their worst fall since March 2020—after the lender uncovered a Rs 590-crore fraud at its Chandigarh branch involving Haryana government-linked accounts. The bank suspended four officials, initiated forensic audits, and filed complaints, while the RBI said the incident poses no systemic risk.

items.title: Omnitech Engineering IPO: 10 key things to know from the RHP ahead of offer launch on Wednesday
items.summary: As Omnitech Engineering prepares for its IPO launch, investors are keen to understand the company's financial health and market position. From revenue growth to strategic plans for the future, discover the key aspects of this upcoming public offering.

items.title: Shree Ram Twistex IPO: Issue receives tepid response on Day 01, subscribed 0.11 times; GMP indicates muted listing
items.summary: Shree Ram Twistex IPO, launched on February 23, has seen a poor response, with a subscription of 0.11 times. The offering, which closes on February 25, aims to raise  ₹110.24 crore for corporate purposes, including setting up a solar power plant. The stock to be listed on both the BSE and NSE.

items.title: Yashhtej Industries IPO allotment finalised: Latest GMP, steps to check allotment status online for the SME IPO
items.summary: Yashhtej Industries allotment date is likely to be finalised today, February 23. Investors can check Yashhtej Industries IPO allotment status online on BSE and the IPO registrar - MAS Services.

items.title: Gold rate today jumps 2%! SIP vs lumpsum: How to make the most of the rise in yellow metal prices? Explained
items.summary: MCX gold prices reclaimed  ₹160,000 per 10-gram mark after consolidating for nearly three weeks. The latest trigger in the gold price rise is the US SC decision on Trump tariffs. The question remains whether lumpsum or SIP investing is a better way to invest in gold now.

items.title: Gainers & Losers: IDFC First Bank, Adani Ports among 6 stocks that were in focus on Monday
items.summary: Indian markets rallied on Monday even as stocks like IDFC First Bank, AU SFB, UPL and HAL saw sharp moves, while Kitex Garments and Adani Ports gained on sector-specific triggers.​

items.title: SME stock inches close to 52-week high after agreement with Japanese company
items.summary: Pramara Promotions share price rose over 2% following a strategic agreement with Sega Corporation to manufacture licensed products for the Indian market. The initial phase includes four products under the 'Crayon Shinchan' edition, enhancing local manufacturing and growth opportunities.

items.title: Nithin Kamath says these hidden charges in trading can wipe out low brokerage advantage
items.summary: Nithin Kamath, co-founder of Zerodha, has urged investors to pay closer attention to Depository Participant (DP) charges, warning that these often-overlooked fees can meaningfully raise trading costs especially when brokers levy them as a percentage of transaction value.

items.title: US Market | Wall Street futures dip as tariff doubts hit risk appetite
items.summary: U.S. stock index futures slipped on Monday as renewed ​tariff uncertainty unnerved investors after President Donald Trump announced a new 15% duty despite a Supreme Court ruling that struck down his broader levies.

items.title: Rupee edges up as local factors eat into US tariff ruling-driven gains
items.summary: The rupee ended at 90.8825 per U.S. dollar, up 0.1% on the day. The currency had opened stronger at 90.76 per dollar on the back of the U.S. Supreme Court striking down President Donald Trump's emergency tariffs, but gave up gains as the session progressed.

items.title: US Market Today | All eyes on big tech earnings as AI anxiety and tariff turmoil keep investors on edge
items.summary: Investors brace for a pivotal week, with Nvidia's earnings at the forefront, seeking AI reassurance amid trade policy shifts.  Tech stocks face scrutiny, while energy and industrials offer support.  Global markets, including India's IT sector, keenly await signals on AI investment and U.S. policy, impacting capital flows and growth outlooks.

items.title: HAL shares decline 5% on Tejas crash reports; company denies incident
items.summary: The shares of Hindustan Aeronautics (HAL) fell 5% to settle at Rs 4,024 on Monday after media reports claimed that a Tejas light combat aircraft (LCA) crashed during a training sortie.

items.title: Carlsberg said to begin $700 million IPO process for India unit
items.summary: Danish brewer Carlsberg is gearing up for a significant move in India. The company is reportedly preparing for a potential listing of its Indian operations. This offering could raise as much as $700 million. Banks have been appointed to advise on the proposed share sale. A filing could happen as early as May.

items.title: Rupee rises 7 paise to close at 90.87 against US dollar
items.summary: The rupee gained 7 paise to settle at 90.87 (provisional) against the US dollar on Monday, aided by a sharp fall in global crude oil prices and a weaker greenback amid renewed global trade-related uncertainties.

items.title: Gold at ₹5,200, oil at $70: Here's what happens next with precious metals and crude
items.summary: Gold and silver are climbing due to policy uncertainty and central bank buying. Crude oil prices remain elevated by geopolitical risks, with a potential $4-$5 premium. Even a limited strike on Iran will not fully remove this risk. OPEC+ is expected to manage supply, supporting prices through mid-year. Investors should watch the Iran-US situation and OPEC+ actions.

items.title: Kiaasa Retail IPO GMP signals 20% listing pop: Check subscription status on Day 1, price band and other details
items.summary: Kiaasa Retail IPO: Kiaasa, an Indian ethnic wear brand, operates over 100 stores in 80 cities and an online platform. Its IPO opened on February 23, with a price band of  ₹121-127 per share. The company aims to use proceeds for new stores and corporate purposes.

items.title: UPL restructuring spooks investors, Street assumes 40% holding company discount
items.summary: Nuvama’s Ebitda estimates for UPL don’t appear to be on the higher side compared to Antique Stock Broking and Motilal Oswal Financial Services. Even the valuation multiples assigned are in sync with those of global peers for each business.

items.title: SBI, Bank Of Baroda among 6 PSU bank stocks that hit 52-week high; rally up to 19% in a month
Shree Ram Twistex IPO Day 1: Retail investors subscribe 82%; check GMP and other details
items.summary: Shree Ram Twistex's Rs 110 crore IPO opened today. Retail investors showed strong interest, subscribing 82% of their portion. Overall subscription is at 9%. The grey market indicates a 6% premium, suggesting modest listing gains. The IPO closes on February 25, with allotment on February 26 and listing on March 2.

items.title: Top Gainers & Losers on Feb 23: Blue Jet Healthcare, Trident, Indian Bank, Siemens, Finolex Cables among top gainers
items.summary: The Indian stock market rose on February 23, with the Nifty 50 gaining 0.52% and Sensex 0.56%, following the US Supreme Court's decision to strike down import levies. Mixed sector performances were noted, with Nifty PSU Bank continuing its upward trend.

items.title: Sebi deploying AI to track influencers and insider trading in real time: Tuhin Kanta Pandey
items.summary: Sebi Chairman Tuhin Kanta Pandey said the regulator is increasingly using artificial intelligence tools to monitor market misconduct, including insider trading, unregistered investment advice, and misleading financial promotions.

items.title: Bitcoin price down $61,000 from peak! Why is Robert Kiyosaki betting big on the cryptocurrency?
items.summary: Robert Kiyosaki increased his Bitcoin investment during a market downturn, purchasing one whole Bitcoin for $67k. He views crashes as opportunities to acquire valuable assets, citing macroeconomic concerns and Bitcoin's fixed supply as key factors in his bullish stance.

items.title: Oil prices fall 1% on US-Iran nuclear talks, Trump's tariff hikes; JM Financial says crude to stay subdued till November
items.summary: Crude oil prices fell by 1% recently due to tariff uncertainties and lower demand, with Brent at $71 per barrel and WTI at $65.75. Analysts expect range-bound action in crude prices amid global uncertainty and OPEC+ output hikes.

items.title: Sebi Chairman Tuhin Kanta Pandey proposes overhaul of PMS regulations
items.summary: The Securities and Exchange Board of India is reviewing its Portfolio Managers Regulations. Chairman Tuhin Kanta Pandey emphasized strengthening investor protection and governance. The PMS industry has seen significant growth in assets and clients. Sebi is also simplifying business processes for portfolio managers. This review aims to keep the framework relevant for evolving market dynamics.

items.title: Sensex jumps 480 points, Nifty 50 ends at 25,713— 10 key highlights from the Indian stock market today
items.summary: The Sensex closed at 83,294.66, rising 480 points, or 0.58%, while the Nifty 50 closed at 25,713, up 142 points, or 0.55%. The BSE 150 MidCap Index fell 0.21%, while the BSE 250 SmallCap Index jumped 0.51%.

items.title: PNGS Reva Diamond Jewellery IPO: From GMP to offer size — Here are 10 things to know ahead of offer launch on Tuesday
items.summary: PNGS Reva Diamond Jewellery IPO is a book-built issue worth  ₹380 crore, comprising entirely a fresh issuance of 0.98 crore equity shares.

items.title: Global Markets | Germany's DAX leads European shares lower on heightened US trade uncertainty
items.summary: European shares dipped in choppy trading on Monday, rocked by a fresh wave of uncertainty regarding U.S. trade ‌policy after ⁠President ⁠Donald Trump announced a new rate of global tariffs.

items.title: Mobilise App Lab IPO sails through first bidding day, issue booked 2.48x so far. Check GMP, issue details, more
items.summary: Mobilise App Lab IPO launched on February 23, with a price band of  ₹75-80 per share. The subscription status is 2.48x, and the company reports a 52% YoY profit increase. Shares list on March 2, with an expected premium in the grey market.

items.title: TCS share price crashes 14% in February so far— Should you buy, sell or hold?
items.summary: TCS shares have fallen over 14% this February due to AI disruption fears and high US interest rates. On Monday, February 23, the IT stock dropped by 1% in intraday trade, despite positive market sentiment.

items.title: Olectra Greentech share price rebounds over 7% from day’s low on  ₹1,800 crore electric bus order
items.summary: Olectra Greentech share price rebounded 7.3% to  ₹1,053.50 after securing a major contract for 1,085 electric buses worth  ₹1,800 crore from TGSRTC. The company’s shares have been under pressure over the last four months, losing a cumulative 30%.

items.title: Silver inventory on COMEX falls below 90 million ounces: What does this mean for investors?
items.summary: Registered silver stocks on COMEX have slipped below the critical 90 million ounce mark to 88,191,059.264 ounces, while eligible inventories declined to 278,065,980.223 ounces, official data showed.

items.title: From IDFC First Bank to Ola Electric Mobility— These are the top most traded stocks on the NSE today
items.summary: IDFC First Bank shares fell 20% after a  ₹590 crore fraud report, while Vodafone Idea dropped 2.6%. Morepen Laboratories surged 19% after securing an  ₹825 crore contract. Ola Electric hit a 52-week low, and Easy Trip Planners saw a 3% decline amid profit booking.

items.title: BPTP partners landowner to develop 11 acre housing project in Faridabad
items.summary: Real estate firm BPTP Ltd has tied up with a landowner to develop an 11-acre housing project in Surajkund, Faridabad, as part of its expansion plan.

items.title: Global Markets | US Supreme Court decision against Trump's tariffs raises uncertainty, but markets stay calm
items.summary: The Supreme Court's ruling against President Trump's tariffs has prompted China and South Korea to assess the implications, while financial markets reacted calmly. Trump proposed new global tariffs under an alternative law, potentially altering trade dynamics for various nations. US officials maintain commitment to existing trade deals despite the legal challenges.

items.title: After Q3 positives, Street’s lofty earnings growth forecasts face reality checks
items.summary: Aggregate sales for the Nifty 500 universe grew 11%, the highest in 11 quarters, mainly led by the benefits of goods and services tax cuts flowing through select sectors.

items.title: GREED & Fear report: Chris Wood of Jefferies says India is emerging as a 'reverse AI trade'
items.summary: India is emerging as a 'reverse AI trade' with a strategic partnership between Anthropic and Infosys to develop custom AI agents. This shift positions Indian IT firms as key enablers of AI adoption despite recent declines in IT stock performance.

items.title: Lodha Developers to invest Rs 1,200 cr on construction in Q4, launch housing projects worth Rs 13K cr
items.summary: Realty firm Lodha Developers Ltd will invest Rs 1,200 crore this quarter on pure construction activities and will launch 12 housing projects with an estimated sales value of nearly Rs 13,000 crore to expand its business.

items.title: One quarter's profit, gone! How IDFC First Bank fell victim to banking's oldest fraud in the age of cyberattacks
items.summary: IDFC First Bank was rocked by a forged cheque fraud at its Chandigarh branch, triggering a sharp market selloff that wiped out over Rs 14,400 crore in shareholder wealth within hours. The issue surfaced on February 18 when Haryana government entities sought to close certain accounts and transfer funds, revealing discrepancies that led to the discovery of internal fraud. Within days, the fallout hit the bank’s 29 lakh shareholders hard.

items.title: 12 stocks see FII stake cuts; shares plunge up to 75%. Are you holding any?
items.summary: Foreign Institutional Investors (FIIs) have reduced their stakes in 12 BSE500 stocks over two quarters, with these shares experiencing significant price drops.  Cohance Lifesciences saw a 75% fall, while Blue Jet Healthcare and Vedant Fashions also dropped over 50%.  This trend highlights potential investor concerns and market performance shifts.

items.title: Indian govt, LIC lose over Rs 1,400 crore in IDFC First Bank market crash
items.summary: IDFC First Bank shares plunged nearly 20% after the lender disclosed a Rs 590 crore fraud at its Chandigarh branch involving accounts linked to Haryana government entities. The sell-off erased about Rs 14,000 crore in market value and triggered Rs 1,452 crore in combined mark-to-market losses for LIC and the Government of India, raising governance concerns.

items.title: Commodity Radar: Gold offers up to Rs 3,400 upside this week. LKP Securities expert lists 5 technical indicators in support
items.summary: Domestic gold prices rallied to Rs 1,60,600 amid global tensions and tariff developments. LKP Securities expects up to Rs 3,400 upside this week, supported by key technical indicators including RSI, MACD, moving averages and Bollinger Bands. Analysts recommend a buy-on-dips strategy with defined support and resistance levels.

items.title: Hitachi Energy, ABB India to Astral: 10 capital goods stocks gain 10–33% in 2026. Do you own any?
items.summary: The capital goods sector, led by Hitachi Energy and Apar Industries, is experiencing strong growth in 2026 due to improved investor sentiment, government spending, and favorable trade conditions. Analysts project continued strength amid healthy ordering trends and margin improvements.

items.title: Sebi will look into stockbrokers’ concerns about RBI’s new funding norms, says chief
items.summary: On 19 February the National Stock Exchange brokers’ forum had approached Sebi, seeking its intervention and a deferment of the RBI's new rules that require banks to tighten funding to proprietary traders under capital market exposure rules.

items.title: HAL share price tumbles 4% as Tejas suffers fresh damage; what’s driving the selloff in other defence stocks?
items.summary: Defence stocks like Bharat Forge and HAL have surged recently, but faced declines due to a HAL Tejas incident triggering profit booking. Analysts indicate that despite short-term corrections, the sector's structural demand outlook remains robust, with significant gains over the past year.

items.title: Silver rebounds 18% from its 2026 low; 4 triggers for next leg of rally
items.summary: Silver prices are staging a strong comeback after a significant fall. Rising tensions between the US and Iran are boosting demand for silver as a safe haven. Global trade uncertainty also plays a role. Analysts point to several key factors that could drive silver prices even higher in the coming days. This metal is showing resilience amid global economic shifts.

items.title: Valuation discipline key as markets navigate tariff noise: Manishi Raychaudhuri
items.summary: Global trade policy volatility is forcing investors to reassess strategies, with market strategist Manishi Raychaudhuri advising a focus on domestic growth. He highlights basic materials, industrials, and consumer discretionary sectors, while cautioning against exporters, consumer staples, and IT services due to valuation and margin concerns.

items.title: Gurugram surpasses Mumbai in ultra-luxury sales in 2025
items.summary: Gurugram led India's luxury housing market in 2025.  Transactions for homes over Rs 10 crore reached Rs 24,120 crore.  This marks a significant jump from 2023.  Gurugram surpassed Mumbai in this segment.  The ultra-luxury market formed 24 percent of the city's total residential value.  New areas are driving this expansion.  Buyer confidence and capital inflows are strong.

items.title: Clean Max Enviro Energy IPO Day 1: Check subscription status, GMP, brokerages’ view & key details
items.summary: CleanMax Enviro Energy Solutions' Rs 3,100 crore IPO opened today, with 3% subscription on Day 1. The issue, priced between Rs 1,000-1,053, includes a fresh issue and an offer for sale. Analysts suggest limited listing gains but see potential for long-term investors due to strong growth prospects in the renewable energy sector.

items.title: Tariff uncertainty, Iran tensions, IT stocks: Treat the turbulence as buying opportunity, says Sameer Dalal
items.summary: Indian markets navigate global uncertainties. Investors should see market turbulence as a buying chance. Tariff noise is manageable, but oil price spikes pose a real threat. Foreign investors continue selling, presenting an exit for them. The IT sector's valuation decline is largely over. Mid-sized private banks show promise despite a recent fraud incident.

items.title: Global Markets | Australian shares dip on U.S. tariff jitters; miners buck the trend
items.summary: Australian shares dipped as US tariff policy uncertainty resurfaced, impacting most sectors. Miners, however, saw gains due to safe-haven demand, with gold stocks also rising. Despite the pullback, strong corporate earnings are expected to limit further declines, with analysts suggesting it's a buying opportunity as the market anticipates a return to record highs.

items.title: Bitcoin slides 5%, slips below $65,000 after Trump’s 15% tariff move
items.summary: Bitcoin and Ethereum experienced significant drops, trading below $65,000 and $2,000 respectively, following the announcement of a 15% global tariff by President Trump. This led to a risk-off sentiment across markets, with major altcoins also slipping and the global crypto market capitalization declining. Analysts suggest Bitcoin may test $60,000 before a rebound, while sentiment gauges indicate extreme fear.

items.title: Global Markets | Tariffs, trade realignment and the new global playbook: What lies ahead for global investors
items.summary: Tariffs are becoming a permanent strategic tool in global trade, driven by US legal shifts and fiscal needs. Countries like India must adapt to redrawn supply chains and renegotiated trade relationships. Expect widespread trade realignment and diversified supply chains by 2026, impacting investment and business strategies.

items.title: Bharat Dynamics shares fall 3% amid selling pressure in defence PSUs
items.summary: Bharat Dynamics shares fell over 3% amid profit booking and broader weakness in defence PSU stocks. Several peers also declined, while select counters saw buying interest. The stock remains under pressure, with weak quarterly earnings and a drop below key moving averages signalling continued caution in the defence segment.

items.title: Up to 65% derating possible! Jefferies downgrades Infosys, TCS, 4 other IT stocks
items.summary: Jefferies has downgraded major Indian IT stocks, including Infosys and TCS, warning of up to 65% valuation downside in a worst-case AI disruption scenario. The brokerage flagged structural risks to growth and margins, while favouring select mid-sized IT firms with stronger growth prospects despite near-term sectoral headwinds.

items.title: Rupee rises 21 paise to 90.73 against the US dollar in early trade
items.summary: The Indian rupee strengthened significantly against the US dollar on Monday. This rise was driven by a sharp decline in global crude oil prices and a weaker dollar. Positive openings in domestic equity markets and increased forex reserves also supported the rupee. The dollar index saw a fall following a US Supreme Court decision on tariffs.

items.title: Textile stocks trade mixed as Gokaldas slips 4%, Kitex rallies 11% on US Supreme Court tariff verdict
items.summary: Textile stocks saw mixed trading as US tariff uncertainties persisted. While some companies like Kitex Garments surged, others faced declines. President Trump's revised global tariffs, invoked under a rarely used trade provision, are set to take effect soon. Meanwhile, a crucial India-US trade negotiation meeting has been rescheduled.

items.title: India bonds stall on geopolitical risk, heavy supply
items.summary: Indian government bonds traded narrowly as geopolitical tensions and a large debt supply weighed on demand. While easing oil price jitters offered some relief, traders remained cautious ahead of significant state and central government debt auctions.  However, indications of potential future rate cuts from the central bank provided a slight boost to sentiment.

items.title: Indian markets show signs of revival as broader market stage comeback: Devang Mehta
items.summary: Indian equity markets show signs of recovery after an 18-month downturn, with improved market breadth and strong earnings growth across segments.  Quality stocks are attracting renewed interest, and PSU banks offer attractive valuations and a dual proxy for India's growth story. The risk-reward balance now favors investors for selective accumulation.

items.title: UPL shares drop 10% after restructuring announcement, Nuvama downgrades to ‘Hold’
items.summary: UPL shares fell 10% after announcing a restructuring to merge global crop protection units into UPL Global, alongside a downgrade by Nuvama to Hold. While the move may unlock value, concerns over leverage and dilution persist. The stock remains under pressure despite neutral technical indicators and ongoing restructuring efforts.
